nodes.label: AI as coding assistant for lookup and explanation
nodes.grounding_span: I've used AI to help me look up ways to code things or to explain how some code works

nodes.label: AI as iterative code refinement partner
nodes.grounding_span: It took some tweaking and back and forth with the AI to be able to get it where I wanted it

nodes.label: AI as delegation tool for specific tasks
AI code debugging difficulty due to unfamiliar code
nodes.grounding_span: It's harder to debug because I didn't write the original code

nodes.label: AI as time-saver for specific tasks
nodes.label_negative: Describing task to AI more time-consuming than doing it myself
nodes.grounding_span: trying to describe what I'm looking for would be harder and more time-consuming than just doing it myself

nodes.label: Skill development and understanding
nodes.grounding_span: knowing what to ask for and describe it is a useful skill

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as coding aid
Frustration with AI debugging cycles
nodes.grounding_span: The going back and forth can be very rewarding and very frustrating

nodes.label: Ambivalence about AI collaboration vs delegation
nodes.grounding_span: I imagine using it more and more...perhaps in the collaborative way that you brought up earlier

nodes.label: Iterative trial-and-error approach
nodes.grounding_span: taking what it gives me, trying it, and then going back and describing any problems

nodes.label: Pragmatic threshold for abandoning AI
nodes.grounding_span: if it ever got to where the AI was just absolutely not giving me what I wanted...that would be the point that I would just write it myself
